steps.title: Find the slant height of each pyramid
steps.content: Both bases are 64 cm², so the base side $s=8$ cm. Slant height uses half the base (4 cm) and pyramid height $h$: $$l=\sqrt{h^2+4^2}$$ Pyramid 1 ($h=12$): $l_1=\sqrt{144+16}=\sqrt{160}\approx12.65$ cm. Pyramid 2 ($h=24$): $l_2=\sqrt{576+16}=\sqrt{592}\approx24.33$ cm.

steps.title: Compute lateral surface areas and their difference
steps.content: Each pyramid has 4 triangular faces with area $\tfrac{1}{2}\cdot8\cdot l=4l$. Lateral area difference: $$4\cdot4(l_2-l_1)=16(\sqrt{592}-\sqrt{160})\approx16(24.33-12.65)\approx16(11.68)\approx186.9\text{ cm}^2$$ The bases are equal so they cancel.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: 187 - closest to 186.9. A is correct.
  • B: 192 - off by about 5. Incorrect.
  • C: 256 - this would be the volume difference, not the surface area difference. Incorrect.
  • D: 453 - this would be the full surface area of pyramid 2, not the difference. Incorrect.
